label: watch
rationale: Strong association with tumor progression and poor prognosis across several cancers, but off-target and developability questions keep it short of a clean GO. A NO-GO would be wrong given the validated biology.

expectedKols.institution: Mater Research Institute, University of Queensland

knownDevelopabilityRisks.note: CDCP1 expression on normal epithelia raises on-target/off-tumor concern.

claimProbes.statement: CDCP1 is proteolytically cleaved, and the cleaved form promotes cancer cell migration and metastasis.
claimProbes.note: Core Hooper-lab biology; should appear grounded in primary literature.

claimProbes.statement: CDCP1 is an approved drug target with a marketed therapy.
claimProbes.note: No approved CDCP1 therapy exists; asserting one is a hallucination.

claimProbes.statement: CDCP1 expression has no relationship to patient prognosis.
